dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 'divergence indicator' to bb_pct_b (Bollinger Band position, 0-1 scale) with thresholds 0.55/0.45 representing upper/lower divergence thresholds. Mapped 'companion series relative channel position' to price_pos_20 (20-bar price position percentile). Entry requires both conditions AND'd; exits use OR logic. Confirmed stop-loss at -1.5% from entry. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction captures that signal generates both LONG (positive divergence) and SHORT (negative divergence) entries.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 'divergence indicator' to bb_pct_b (Bollinger Band position, 0-1 scale) with thresholds 0.55/0.45 representing upper/lower divergence thresholds. Mapped 'companion series relative channel position' to price_pos_20 (20-bar price position percentile). Entry requires both conditions AND'd; exits use OR logic. Confirmed stop-loss at -1.5% from entry. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction captures that signal generates both LONG (positive divergence) and SHORT (negative divergence) entries.

source_rule_text: A trader should initiate bullish positions when a companion series shows positive divergence (higher relative channel position than benchmark) and bearish positions when negative divergence exists (lower relative channel position than benchmark), using threshold-based signals from a divergence indicator.
